div1.entryFree: Weaver, one whose occupation is to weave: Wiv. V, 1, 24 (cf. 1 Samuel XVII, 7). Mids. I, 2, 19. III, 1, 23. H6B IV, 2, 30. H8 I, 2, 33. --s supposed to be good singers and particularly given to singing psalms (being most of them Calvinists and refugees from the Netherlands): a catch that will draw three souls out of one w. Tw. II, 3, 61. I would I were a w., I could sing psalms or any thing, H4A II, 4, 147.
